description: This figure presents Mott-Schottky curves of hot-rolled Fe–30Mn–8Al–1C low-density steel samples with different Cu contents: 0 wt% Cu (0Cu, pink square line), 1 wt% Cu (1Cu, blue circle line), and 3 wt% Cu (3Cu, green triangle line). The x-axis represents potential versus saturated calomel electrode (V_SCE) ranging from -1.0 V to 1.0 V, and the y-axis represents C⁻² in units of 10⁹ F²·cm⁻⁴. Three potential regions (I, II, III) are divided. The peak value of C⁻² decreases gradually as Cu content increases, while the corresponding peak potential shifts slightly, indicating that carrier density in the passive film increases with higher Cu addition. These results align with the trend that corrosion resistance of the steel reduces with increased Cu content, supporting the conclusion that Cu alloying deteriorates the corrosion performance of Fe-Mn-Al-C low-density steel in 3.5 wt% NaCl solution.
